es.meaning: Not at all (with negation); at all.
es.exampleTranslation: I don't like it at all.

it.meaning: Not at all (with negation); at all.
it.exampleTranslation: I don't like it at all.

bridge.note: Italian has one 'for' (per); Spanish split it in two — and this phrase falls on the para side. Meanwhile por nada drifted off to mean 'you're welcome' in much of Latin America. One preposition choice, two different conversations.

interference.es_to_it: The per/por/para minefield in one phrase: it's PARA nada, not 'por nada' — which in LatAm is what you say for 'you're welcome'.
interference.it_to_es: Mirror trap: 'per nulla/per niente' is right in Italian, but exporting 'por nada' into a Spanish negation lands on the wrong phrase entirely.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm.
